title: Neither war nor peace: Trump unilaterally extends ceasefire with Iran
subhead: The US president has not set a new deadline for Iran, instead waiting for the various political and security factions within the Iranian regime to reach a 'unified proposal.'

title: Lebanon displays proximity to France ahead of talks with Israel
London hosts military talks on France and UK's Hormuz mission
France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help: 'The mineralized bones literally are exploding'
subhead: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.

title: Why colonial-era Congo archives in Belgium are caught in a standoff over strategic minerals
subhead: Accusations of 'digital colonialism' and capitalist predation are at the heart of a standoff between an American mining company and a Belgian public museum, as interest in the Democratic Republic of Congo's vast mineral wealth intensifies.

title: In 'Michael,' Michael Jackson is depicted only as musical phenomenon, far from accusations
subhead: Antoine Fuqua's biopic sidesteps any controversy by focusing on the singer's early career, but stands out for the way it recreates the electricity of his live performances.

title: 'Bookshops now serve as sentinels of resistance to the radical rightward shift of society'
subhead: Christian Thorel, a prominent figure in independent book selling, was among the first to sound the alarm about billionaire Vincent Bolloré's ideological grip on the publishing sector, starting with the acquisition of France's top publishing group Hachette, in 2023.

'Why am I alive?': The Yazidi genocide as told by one of its survivors
subhead: Samih Taha, now a refugee in France, is one of the few survivors of the Islamic State group massacre of his village in northern Iraq on August 15, 2014. Then 17 years old, he went into debt to buy back his mother and sisters, who had been forced into sexual slavery. He told Le Monde his story and the tragedy that befell his family.

title: Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
subhead: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

title: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhead: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse
